page: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance within the Cloud GPU family. It features 2 shared Intel Broadwell x86_64 vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and a 50 GB NVMe SSD. The instance is equipped with a fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU, providing 2 GB of dedicated VRAM, which represents a 0.125 slice of a full A16 GPU. It also includes one complimentary public IPv4 address.

Performance benchmarks indicate a clear division between CPU and GPU capabilities. The shared Intel processor delivers weak multi-core performance and average single-core performance, as shown by stress-ng and Passmark CPU scores. Memory bandwidth is generally weak, and database operations on Passmark are rated poor. However, the fractional NVIDIA A16 GPU excels at lightweight AI tasks, achieving top-tier performance in LLM inference prompt processing for small 135M models at 7078.28 tokens per second and average performance for medium 7B models.

This instance offers a cost-efficient entry point for hardware-accelerated workloads that do not require a full dedicated GPU. The resource density is tailored for low-intensity GPU tasks, though users face tradeoffs in CPU-bound performance and memory bandwidth. It is best suited for lightweight LLM inference, small-scale model testing, basic graphics rendering, and entry-level CUDA development.
description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance designed for entry-level hardware-accelerated workloads. It features two shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs clocked at 2.0 GHz, 8.0 GB of system memory, and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage. The primary differentiator is its fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU, which provides 2 GB of VRAM. While CPU-bound benchmarks and memory bandwidth are relatively weak, the instance delivers top-tier performance for small-scale LLM prompt processing and average speeds for medium-sized models. This configuration offers a highly cost-efficient option for developers needing GPU acceleration without the expense of a dedicated card. It is best suited for lightweight AI inference, CUDA development, and small-scale model testing.
og_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance featuring 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, a 50 GB NVMe SSD, and a 0.125 slice of an NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB VRAM for entry-level AI workloads.
meta_description: Vultr vcg-a16-2c-8g-2vram is a fractional GPU instance with 2 shared Intel vCPUs, 8 GB RAM, and a 2 GB NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU for budget-friendly AI tasks.
tagline: An entry-level fractional GPU instance combining shared Intel processors with NVIDIA Ampere acceleration for budget-conscious AI inference and development.
bullet_points: Fractional NVIDIA Ampere A16 GPU with 2 GB of dedicated VRAM
Two shared Intel Broadwell vCPUs running at 2.0 GHz
8.0 GB of system memory and 50 GB of local NVMe SSD storage
Top-tier prompt processing speeds for small-scale LLM inference
Cost-efficient profile for entry-level CUDA development and testing
